top_stories.title: Flaws in Passkey Implementation Show Old Attacks Still Work
top_stories.summary: Researchers discovered exploitable flaws in Microsoft's passkey implementation that could allow attackers to impersonate privileged users. The findings, presented ahead of Black Hat USA, demonstrate that legacy attack techniques remain effective against modern passkey deployments.

top_stories.title: Device Code Phishing: Turning a Convenience Feature Into an MFA Bypass
top_stories.summary: Device code phishing exploits a legitimate OAuth 2.0 authentication flow designed for input-limited devices to bypass MFA protections. The article details the attack mechanism, a recently observed case, and defensive countermeasures organizations can implement.

top_stories.title: Attackers exploit critical Check Point flaw to take over firewall management (CVE-2026-16232)
top_stories.summary: Attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-16232, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Check Point Security Management and Multi-Domain Security Management servers. An unauthenticated attacker can obtain an application login token to access SmartConsole with full admin privileges, enabling changes to security policy and configuration.

top_stories.title: Russian Espionage Group Exploited Zimbra Zero-Day to Steal Mail and 2FA Codes
top_stories.summary: A Russian state-sponsored espionage group exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Zimbra's webmail client to steal emails, browser-saved passwords, and two-factor authentication recovery codes from Western organizations. The NSA and CISA published an advisory on the campaign, which required only opening a malicious message to trigger the payload targeting the last 90 days of email and full email directories.

top_stories.title: How Synthetic Identity Fraud is Coming for Machine Identities
top_stories.summary: This article examines synthetic identity fraud as it applies to machine identities, where attackers fabricate new identities by combining real and fictitious data points rather than stealing existing credentials. The concept extends traditional synthetic identity fraud techniques into the machine identity domain, an area of growing concern for IAM professionals managing non-human identities.

news.title: FedRAMP Rev5 Is Ending: What the 20x Transition Really Requires
news.summary: FedRAMP is transitioning from Rev5 to a new "20x" framework that replaces point-in-time assessments with continuous, machine-readable security control evidence. The article from Anecdotes outlines what this shift means for organizations seeking or maintaining FedRAMP authorization, including implications for how security controls are demonstrated and validated.

news.title: Google Adds Selfie Video Recovery for Users Locked Out of Their Accounts
news.summary: Google announced a new account recovery method that allows users to submit a selfie video to regain access to locked accounts. The feature serves as an additional option alongside existing recovery methods such as email address or phone number verification.

news.title: Product Showcase: AppViewX Agent Identity Security
news.summary: AppViewX has released a product focused on agent identity security, addressing the growing challenge of securing non-human/machine identities in enterprise environments. The solution targets AI-driven autonomous agents and quantum computing threats to cryptographic trust, positioning itself in the machine identity management space.

news.title: Police Dismantle Kratos Phishing Kit Built to Steal Microsoft 365 Sessions and Bypass MFA
news.summary: German and US law enforcement dismantled the infrastructure of the Kratos phishing kit, which was designed to steal Microsoft 365 sessions and bypass MFA, while Indonesian authorities arrested its alleged developer. The kit was described by German investigators as one of the world's most widely used criminal phishing tools targeting enterprise authentication systems.

news.title: Microsoft Entra ID to restrict and retire custom CSS for branded sign-ins
news.summary: Microsoft is restricting and retiring custom CSS support in Entra ID's branded sign-in pages as part of its Secure Future Initiative. The changes limit CSS positioning properties to prevent potential phishing abuse enabled by unrestricted styling capabilities.

news.title: Disable SSO permission prompts in Windows 11
news.summary: Microsoft introduced a new registry policy in Windows 11 that allows administrators to automatically approve SSO permission prompts on managed devices. The change was driven by regulatory updates in the European Economic Area (EEA) that previously required explicit user consent for SSO permissions.

news.title: Hybrid identity turns into technical debt for cloud-only Microsoft environments
news.summary: Hybrid identity configurations involving on-premises Active Directory and Entra Connect are creating technical debt for organizations running Microsoft Entra ID, Microsoft 365, and Intune. The friction arises from legacy AD domain controllers clashing with modern cloud-native workloads, complicating identity management in cloud-only or cloud-first environments.

news.title: Closing the Identity Gaps in Critical Infrastructure Security
news.summary: Specops Software published guidance on applying Zero Trust principles to critical infrastructure security. The piece focuses on verifying both user identities and device trust as a defense against attacks that begin with stolen credentials, compromised devices, or trusted accounts.

news.title: Teleport Establishes Agent Trust with New Identity Security Capabilities
news.summary: Teleport has launched new identity security capabilities including Session Summaries, Agentic Classifiers, and Risk Scoring to establish trust for AI agents. The features are designed to identify and prevent agent misalignment, targeting agentic identity security use cases.

news.title: Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk Services Platform
news.summary: A vulnerability in Rockwell Automation's FactoryTalk Services Platform has been disclosed that allows attackers to impersonate authorized users on the FTSP server. Successful exploitation results in unauthorized access to system configurations, making it relevant to authentication and access control in industrial control systems.

news.title: Securing ASP.NET Core APIs with Microsoft Entra ID Authentication
news.summary: The article covers how to secure ASP.NET Core APIs using Microsoft Entra ID authentication. It addresses configuration steps, authentication implementation, and best practices for API security using Microsoft's identity platform.

news.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
news.summary: Identity-based attacks have surpassed exploits as the leading root cause of ransomware incidents. MFA was present in 97% of credential-based attacks yet failed to prevent compromise, highlighting critical gaps in current authentication defenses.

news.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
news.summary: CISA released a postmortem report on a data breach in which a contractor accidentally exposed dozens of internal credentials, including AWS GovCloud keys, in a public GitHub repository for nearly six months. The incident was not discovered internally but was instead reported to CISA by KrebsOnSecurity, highlighting gaps in credential monitoring and secrets management practices.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and phishing to bypass MFA and compromise identity systems.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords, temporarily seizing high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force Chief Master Sergeant's profiles. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to bypass normal account authentication and force password resets were distributed via Telegram.
